headline: CSK need to win 8 of 11 remaining. Historical survival from 0-3: 12%.
text: At 0-3 with a league-worst -2.467 NRR, CSK sit 4 points behind the safety line of 16. They need an 8-3 run from here — a 73% win rate — when their season win rate is 0%. Only 2 of 17 teams in IPL history have qualified after starting 0-3. The math is terminal.

headline: PBKS's NRR lead is invisible armour. Here's why it matters now.
text: PBKS (+1.233) have a 3.7 NRR gap over 4th-placed RR. That gap is worth roughly half a win in tiebreaker scenarios. If the top 4 ends congested at 14-16 points — which the fixture density suggests — PBKS can afford to lose close games and still qualify. Nobody else has that cushion.

headline: Sameer Rizvi is doing at #4 what DC paid Marsh ₹15Cr to do.
text: Rizvi: 160 runs, SR 152, middle-overs (6-15) SR 158. Marsh: 42 runs from 3 innings, SR 112. DC's ₹50L uncapped find is outperforming their marquee overseas bat in exactly the role he was bought for — the middle-overs accumulator who can shift gears. The scouting department deserves a raise.

headline: Klaasen's 145 runs are masking SRH's structural bowling failure.
text: Heinrich Klaasen: 145 runs, SR 161, 2nd in Orange Cap. Travis Head: SR 190. Yet SRH are 1-2. When your two overseas superstars are both firing and you're still losing, the diagnosis is clear: domestic bowling. SRH's 5th bowler economy (death): 14.2. That's not fixable by selection.

headline: Jacob Duffy is quietly the most important player in the title defense.
text: RCB's NRR (+2.415) is league-best. Everyone credits the batters — Padikkal's 111, Kohli's SR 181. But Duffy's powerplay economy of 5.50 with 5 wickets is what's creating the platform. RCB have historically choked when the new ball doesn't work. Duffy has removed that vulnerability entirely.

headline: Captains are 0-for-12 after winning the toss. The chasing meta is dead.
text: 10 of 12 toss winners chose to field. Win rate: 0%. This isn't a sample size fluke at 12 matches — it's a structural shift. Scoreboard pressure in 2026 is different. First captain to bet on batting first gains an edge nobody else has noticed.

headline: Archer's 4.75 economy vs Rohit's 205 SR — Guwahati's 6-over war.
text: Tonight's RR vs MI clash has the best powerplay matchup of the tournament. Archer has dismissed Rohit twice in their last 4 IPL meetings, both in the first 3 overs. But Guwahati's shorter square boundaries (55m) favour Rohit's pull shot — his highest-SR scoring zone. Venue geometry versus historical dominance. I'm backing Archer.

headline: The Situation Room says CSK are dead. The scouting says they're broken. I say: wait.
text: CSK's auction strategy specifically built a team to peak after match 7. Samson, Gaikwad, and Dube have historically been April failures and May monsters — combined SR jumps from 118 to 149 after the season's first third. CSK aren't panicking because 0-3 was survivable in their model. The question is whether the NRR hole is too deep.
